title: [kimi-review] cf7526a: [SEVERITY: medium] The change is behaviorally a no-op. After
description:
Commit: cf7526a08
Message: fix(proto): handle signed int32 for rx_rssi in MeshPacket decode

Finding: [SEVERITY: medium] The change is behaviorally a no-op. After `value & 0xFFFFFFFF`, value is in [0, 2³²−1], so `value & 0x80000000` (truthy check) and `value > 0x7FFFFFFF` are exactly equivalent conditions. If there was a real bug decoding negative `rx_rssi` values, this commit does not fix it — the actual bug likely lives elsewhere (e.g., `_varint_to_int32`
notes: Commit: 3bbdda33c (addressed kimi-review on signed int32 decode with improved _varint_to_int32 + QueueStatus.from_bytes + test)
